description: This figure presents a schematic diagram illustrating the evolution of true stress-true strain curves for low-alloy steel under varying thermomechanical deformation conditions, specifically demonstrating the effect of transient changes in the Zener-Hollomon parameter (Z_p) during hot compression. The graph is divided into two stages: Stage I (Before variation) shows the initial deformation behavior up to strain ε_I, while Stage II (After variation) depicts the stress response after a sudden change in deformation conditions. Four distinct curves are plotted: black dashed line for constant low Z_p1, purple dashed line for constant high Z_p2 (where Z_p2 > Z_p1), blue solid line for decreasing Z_p from Z_p2 to Z_p1 at ε_I, and red solid line for increasing Z_p from Z_p1 to Z_p2 at ε_I. Key stress parameters are labeled including initial yield stresses (σ_01, σ_02), critical stresses for dynamic recrystallization (σ_c1, σ_c2), peak stresses (σ_p2, σ_p2^(v)), and steady-state stresses (σ_ss1, σ_ss2). The schematic demonstrates that a decrease in Z_p (blue curve) causes an abrupt stress drop followed by gradual softening to a new steady state, while an increase in Z_p (red curve) leads to stress elevation and subsequent work hardening. This figure supports the core scientific conclusion that transient variations in deformation conditions induce non-continuous flow stress responses governed by the competition between work hardening, dynamic recovery, and dynamic recrystallization mechanisms, which is fundamental for understanding multi-stage recrystallization behavior in metallic materials.
